summary: Updated repository tooling to match newer dtk-template conventions. The PR was mostly Python formatting and lint cleanup, with small type/path handling fixes in build tooling: .flake8 now ignores E203 as well as E501, tool scripts were reformatted to Black-style double quotes and line wrapping, ninja_syntax now stringifies escaped implicit/order-only/implicit-output paths before extending build-line lists, and project.py now stores LinkStep inputs as strings while accepting os.PathLike values. No review discussion or PR body was present in the slice.
what_changed: .flake8 changed from ignoring only E501 to ignoring both E203 and E501, with comments documenting E203 as whitespace before ':' and E501 as line too long.
tools/find_include.py was reformatted: added blank lines around top-level functions, switched string literals to double quotes, and wrapped the argparse description across multiple lines.
tools/git_catchup.py was reformatted: added a blank line before the top-level function, switched set/string literals and git checkout separator to double quotes, and wrapped argparse setup lines.
tools/link.py wrapped the argparse constructor and path argument definition for style consistency.
tools/ninja_syntax.py reordered typing imports and changed build() list extension for implicit inputs, order-only inputs, and implicit outputs to use map(str, ...).

tools/project.py introduced progress_path earlier near build_path, changed LinkStep.add to accept os.PathLike and append str(obj), removed an unnecessary f-string for preplf_ldflags, changed 'not step.name in generated_rels' to 'step.name not in generated_rels', and removed a later duplicate progress_path assignment.
tools/split_suggester.py removed an extra blank line immediately inside ProgramError.
smart_moves: Kept the template update small and mechanical, limiting risk to tooling-only files.
Adjusted .flake8 to ignore E203, which is commonly needed when using Black-style formatting because Black can intentionally produce slices that flake8 would otherwise flag.
Made ninja_syntax build-line construction more robust by converting escaped paths to strings before extending command token lists.
Made project.py LinkStep.add accept path-like objects but normalize stored inputs to strings, preserving downstream expectations while allowing pathlib-style callers.
Hoisted progress_path near build_path in generate_build_ninja, reducing repeated local setup and grouping related output paths together.
decomp_lessons: Template-sync PRs can contain both formatting churn and small functional robustness fixes; reviewers and future agents should separate mechanical style updates from behavior-affecting path/type changes.
When Python tooling migrates toward pathlib usage, normalize path-like values at boundaries that feed string-oriented systems such as Ninja files.
For generated Ninja syntax, ensure all path tokens are serialized to strings before joining or extending mixed lists.
Align flake8 settings with the formatter actually used by the repository to avoid style-only failures blocking decomp work.
Prefer idiomatic Python expressions such as 'x not in collection' over 'not x in collection' in shared tooling.
naming_conventions: The PR preserved existing snake_case Python names such as search_string_in_files, checkout_except, generate_build_ninja, progress_path, build_path, and build_tools_path.
Path variables in project.py continue to use the '_path' suffix, e.g. build_path, progress_path, preplf_path, and plf_path.
Argument parser metavars remain explicit for CLI-facing source paths, e.g. C_FILE in tools/link.py.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching or C decompilation tactic was present; the changes were repository tooling/template maintenance.
Indirectly supports matching workflow by keeping build/link/progress tooling and helper scripts lint-clean and compatible with path-like inputs.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR merged quickly on the same day it was opened, but the slice does not provide reviewer rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching tools/ninja_syntax.py map(str) path serialization to see whether this template change fixed build failures.
Search for dtk-template update PRs to compare repeated formatting/lint conventions across the project.
Search for LinkStep.add callers passing pathlib.Path or os.PathLike objects after this PR.
